playbook.intent: Ship a regulated-domain feature → merged PR + evidence pack + traceable to control catalogue + AI agent did not invent unsafe patterns.
playbook.scope: Synthesis playbook: a single feature in a regulated domain (FinTech payment flow, HIPAA PHI handling, PCI cardholder data path) shipped with a defensible audit trail. 'Done' = merged PR, evidence pack attached, traceable to client control catalogue, AI agent contained.

success-criteria.criterion: PR merged with control IDs in description
Threat model updated as code where relevant

stage.name: Spec to control
stage.intent: Map the feature to controls before any code.

tasks.task: Map feature to client control IDs
Identify versioning impact for any external consumer

stage.decision-gate: Advance only when every AC is mapped to a control ID.

stage.name: Design for audit
stage.intent: Choose patterns that an auditor likes.

tasks.task: Apply clean-architecture and DDD where boundaries matter
Apply API gateway patterns at security perimeter

stage.decision-gate: Advance only after threat model has been peer-reviewed.

stage.name: Build + audit-grade review
stage.intent: Code the change with reviewable guard rails.

tasks.task: Use MR-graph vs diff reviewer where the platform supports
Run government/license compliance scan in CI
Audit-grade code review on the resulting PR

stage.decision-gate: Advance only when review references control IDs.

stage.name: Evidence + ship
stage.intent: Attach evidence the auditor will pull later.

tasks.task: Run SOC 2 evidence generator if available
Attach evidence pack to the PR
Update living docs for the new control posture
Merge with control IDs in the squash message

stage.decision-gate: Ship only when evidence is attached AND merge message references control IDs.

extra.value: [Full audit prep — separate playbook, Architecture migration — single feature, Net-new compliance program — uses existing regime]
[Compliance regime active (SOC 2 / PCI / HIPAA / banking), Client control catalogue accessible, AI guardrails active in the engagement]

[Treating compliance as a post-merge concern — evidence gets reconstructed from memory, AI-generated code with no attestation — auditors disallow it implicitly, Threat models stored as ad-hoc diagrams — drift before next sprint]
['Could the auditor pull this PR and trace to a control in <60s?', 'Did we attest AI involvement explicitly?', 'Did the threat model survive review by someone outside the change?']

{human: faion get-content compliance-grade-feature-delivery --format md, agent: faion get-content compliance-grade-feature-delivery --format context, json: faion get-content compliance-grade-feature-delivery --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: 'Initial draft for F-064 P4 outsource-specialist persona, synthesis angle'}]
